name: Springtime Ham with Rhubarb Sauce
recipeIngredient: 3 cup Rhubarb, fresh OR 16-oz frozen cut rhubarb
recipeInstructions:
1. Do not preheat the oven.
2. Place the ham, straight from the refrigerator, on a rack in a shallow roasting pan. Add 1/2 cup water.
3. Insert a meat thermometer into the thickest part of the ham.
4. Cover the pan tightly with aluminum foil, leaving the thermometer dial exposed.
5. Roast in an oven set to 325°F until the thermometer registers 135°F, about 19-23 minutes per pound.
6. Meanwhile, combine rhubarb, sugar, orange juice, orange peel, dry mustard, and cinnamon stick in a large saucepan.
7. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium and cook, uncovered, for about 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. Remove the cinnamon stick.
9. After 15 minutes before the end of cooking time, remove the aluminum foil from the ham and spoon a small amount of sauce over it.
10. Remove the ham when the meat thermometer registers 135°F. Allow to stand, covered, for about 10 more minutes, or until the thermometer registers 140°F.
11. Serve the remaining sauce with the ham. Makes 2 1/4 cups sauce.
